facts.report_excerpt: = Messaging Report :description: Current owner-oriented maturity report for the Messaging component. :page-slug: messaging-report This page is the current owner-oriented maturity report for *Messaging*. It is written not as a defect log, but as a public-facing readiness portrait: what the component already demonstrates, what maturity band it currently occupies, and which leader-class direction it is converging toward. == Component Portrait Messaging is not shaped as a simple chat widget layer or a narrow transport helper. It reads as a Symfony-oriented message runtime and realtime messaging component: a system intended to manage message application runtime, API and GraphQL exposure, event publication, Redis-backed coordination, realtime delivery, and governed operational boot discipline. The component already shows signs of being treated as a governed subsystem rather than as a convenience feature. Its current shape suggests an internal conversation and realtime-delivery platform layer designed for durability, structured evolution, and integration into a wider ecosystem. == Ecosystem Role Within the broader Smartresponsor direction, Messaging appears to occupy the role of a conversation runtime and realtime message-delivery component. Its ecosystem role can be understood across several functions: * as a message and conversation surface, * as an API and GraphQL exposure layer, * as a realtime event-distribution surface, * as a Redis-backed coordination and rate-control layer, * and as a reusable messaging runtime capability for broader systems. This makes Messaging strategically more important than a typical chat feature. It points toward a reusable internal messaging capability that other components can depend on. == Current Maturity Messaging already presents several strong signals of maturity: * a clear message-centric product identity, * explicit PHP 8.4 targeting and Symfony 7.4/8.0 compatibility, * API Platform, GraphQL, Mercure, Messenger, Redis, and React Socket breadth, * multiple runtime bootstrap and diagnostic scripts, * a boot-ledger operating surface, * and a governed runtime around Docker, preflight, install, about, and test checks. In its current public reading, the component is no longer in a prototype-only band. It already behaves like a structured component moving toward release-candidate maturity. == RC Scorecard [cols="2,1,4",options="header"] |=== | Layer | Score to RC | Reading | Product identity | 8.6 / 10 | The component is already clearly framed around Message as a Symfony application rather than a generic communication helper. | Architecture and domain shape | 8.3 / 10 | The repository shape points toward a coherent conversation runtime and event-delivery surface, even where the visible domain sample is still runtime-heavy rather than entity-heavy. | Runtime and application surface | 8.9 / 10 | API Platform, GraphQL, Mercure, Redis, Messenger, React Socket, HTTP runtime, and Docker bootstrap posture already establish a serious messaging application layer. | QA maturity | 8.3 / 10 | The quality contour is solid across PHPUnit, Panther, linting, cs, phpstan, and runtime test helpers. | Governance and operational readiness | 9.0 / 10 | Preflight, install-runtime, about-runtime, test-runtime, and boot-ledger flows form one of the strongest runtime-governance surfaces among the components. | Realtime posture | 8.8 / 10 | Redis, Mercure, React Socket, GraphQL, and runtime orchestration signal a genuine realtime and event-oriented direction. | Market-facing alignment | 8.4 / 10 | The component already aligns well with enterprise conversation platform and realtime messaging infrastructure classes, though it still reads more as a governed platform core than a finished public messaging product shell. | Overall readiness to RC | 8.6 / 10 | Messaging is already a strong pre-RC component with clear enterprise-class internal conversation and realtime-runtime signals. |=== == Leader Line Messaging should not be measured against simple contact forms, email helpers, or lightweight chat widgets. Its natural comparison line is closer to the enterprise-class conversation platform and realtime messaging infrastructure category, including the kind of capability bands represented by: * conversation and channel-based message handling, * API and GraphQL message exposure, * realtime publish-subscribe delivery, * presence-adjacent runtime coordination, * and governed operational handling of messaging infrastructure. This means the component is better understood as targeting the same strategic class of problem addressed by stronger messaging and realtime leaders, while expressing that class through a Symfony-native internal component. In that sense, the leader line is not another chat add-on. It is closer to: * enterprise conversation-platform class, * enterprise realtime messaging class, * enterprise messaging-runtime and event-distribution class, * reinterpreted as an internal ecosystem component. == …

verdict.summary: Messaging presents as a governed Symfony-based messaging runtime with strong runtime scripts, API/GraphQL surface, and observability hooks; primary gap is visible CI/release automation in-repo.
strengths: Symfony 7.4/8.0 app with API Platform, GraphQL, Messenger, Mercure, Redis, Doctrine breadth
Clear runtime governance via preflight/install/test/boot-ledger scripts
OpenAPI contract present for external surface
Observability and tracing hooks (Prometheus rules, OTEL/tracing service configs)
Solid test/tooling posture (PHPUnit, PHPStan, Panther; Playwright script scaffolding)
Structured docs assets (README, ROADMAP, owner-facing Antora report)
risks: No committed CI workflows found in-repo
.dist-only PHPUnit config may require local setup for contributors
No git tags detected for release/version governance
Antora navigation/index files absent, which may limit docs surfacing
next_actions: Add CI workflow to run preflight, lint, PHPStan, PHPUnit/Panther, and Playwright on PRs and main
Adopt semantic version tags and release notes to formalize cut lines
Document deployment/operations runbook linking Prometheus rules and dashboards
Add Antora nav/index entries to surface the component report in site navigation
Pin a minimal supported PHP/Symfony matrix in CI to guard ^8.4 target and 7.4/8.0 compatibility
notes: Scores anchored to the current owner-facing report and repo scan; minor adjustments reflect absent CI and tagging.
Composer targets PHP ^8.4 while local runtime may vary; ensure delivery images/environments align.

product.summary: Component identity and market-facing shape remain materially present, reinforced by README/ROADMAP and an OpenAPI contract that frames Messaging as a Symfony-native messaging runtime.
risks: Assessment seeded from current pre-RC atlas and should be enriched by regular repository scans.
improvements: Refresh this metric from a scheduled repository assessment run.

architecture.summary: Symfony-oriented architecture shape is materially present, with clear service modularization for realtime, observability, security, and runtime layers.
risks: Assessment seeded from current pre-RC atlas and should be enriched by regular repository scans.
improvements: Refresh this metric from a scheduled repository assessment run.

runtime.summary: Runtime and application surface are materially present via API Platform/GraphQL, Messenger, Mercure, Redis, and governed bootstrap/test/ledger scripts.
risks: Assessment seeded from current pre-RC atlas and should be enriched by regular repository scans.
improvements: Refresh this metric from a scheduled repository assessment run.

qa.summary: Testing and QA posture are materially present with PHPUnit, PHPStan, Panther, and Playwright scripts; CI automation is not yet visible in-repo.
risks: No committed CI workflows found to run tests on PRs/merges.
improvements: Add a CI workflow to execute PHPStan, PHPUnit/Panther, and Playwright suites on pushes/PRs.

ops.summary: Operational, governance, and security posture are strong with preflight/install/test/boot-ledger flows and observability rules; release/CI automation is the primary visible gap.
risks: No committed CI workflows found for delivery automation.
No git tags detected for release/version governance.
improvements: Add CI workflows for build/test/release and artifact publishing.
Adopt semantic version tags and maintain CHANGELOG for releases.

market.summary: Business- and portfolio-facing alignment is materially present; the component reads as an internal conversation/realtime platform capability with a defined API surface.
risks: Assessment seeded from current pre-RC atlas and should be enriched by regular repository scans.
improvements: Refresh this metric from a scheduled repository assessment run.

overall.summary: Overall release-candidate direction remains visible and strong; absent CI and tagging slightly temper immediate RC readiness.
risks: No committed CI workflows and no tags reduce near-term RC confidence.
improvements: Introduce CI and versioned releases to close the final governance gap.
